rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHERINE ESTHER HINES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/26/2021 at 1:30pm. Issues: 1) Petition must be amended to only include a two year account. [LRSF 14.35 A.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DIANA M. LEW TRUST
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Trustee Maria Matsu's Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication us DENIED. The record on this motion contains substantial evidence of the following: Settlor Diana M. Lew executed the Diana M. Lew Trust, dated March 21, 2017 ("Trust"). (Ptr's Separate Statement ("PSS") #1.) On April 9, 2019, Lew executed a Power of Attorney designating Maria Matsu as her attorney-in-fact. (Fung Decl. 3 & Exhs. A, B & C.) On April 25, Matsu, as attorney-in-fact for Lew, executed the First Amendment to Declaration of Trust for Diana M. Lew Initially Created March 21, 2017 ("First Amendment"). (See PSS #7.) Four days later, Lew died, the Trust became irrevocable and Matsu became trustee. (PSS #2, 5.) The next day, Matsu as trustee of the Trust served Petitioner with a Probate Code section 16061.7 Notification informing him that the Rust had become irrevocable and enclosing the 2017 Trust instrument, the First Amendment, and the POA. (PSS #2, 5.) Within 120 days of the Notification, Petitioner filed his Petition to Invalidate Trust Amendment ("Petition") seeking to invalidate the First Amendment, arguing that: (1) it is invalid under Probate Code section 15401(c) because the trust instrument did not authorize modifications or revocation by an attorney-in-fact; and (2) it is void because it's the result of Matsu's undue influence. A beneficiary under all purported trust instruments, Petitioner did better under the 2017 Trust and worse under the First Amendment. On September 21, 2019, Matsu served a second Section 16061.7 Notification apparently correcting her April 30 Notification, this time enclosing a "Proposed Diana Lew's Trust Amendment as of 4/24/19" dated April 25, 2019 ("Proposed Amendment"). (PSS #6.) On September 26, 2019, Matsu filed her Objections and Response to the Petition ("Objections & Response"). [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DIANA M. LEW TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Matsu focuses on the September 21 Notification and the Proposed Amendment and argues that Petitioner's challenge is moot because the Proposed Amendment controls and any challenge to the Proposed Amendment is now barred by the 120-day limitation in Probate Code sections 16061.7 and 16061.8 and "relation back" is unavailable as a matter of law. Matsu failed to carry her burden. Petitioner brought a timely challenge to the Trust in reliance on Matsu's April 30 Notification. (Opposing Separate Statement 2, 6-8; see Probate C., section 16061.7, subd. (a).) Matsu has not shown that Petitioner was legally obligated to amend his Petition or file a new petition following receipt of her second notification and her new evidence regarding the operative trust instrument. In fact, it is not clear that the September 21 Notification was even authorized under Section 16061.7 in that no triggering event occurred after April 30 (the date of the original notification). (See Probate C., section 16061.7, subd. (a).) These legal shortcomings aside, the court cannot summarily adjudicate either claim in light of the material disputes of fact, not least of which is the dispute evidenced by Matsu's two notifications, i.e., whether the operative trust instrument is: (a) the 2017 instrument; (b) the 2017 instrument plus the First Amendment; o,r (c) the 2017 instrument plus the Proposed Amendment. These and other issues must be resolved by the trier of fact. The court need not reach the issue of relation back. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on January 14, 2021. Petitioner Fung is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]
